inputs.label: Excavation Length
inputs.businessContext: Length of excavation area. Based on civil engineering standards (e.g., ASTM D3740).

inputs.label: Excavation Width
inputs.businessContext: Width of excavation area. Common in earthwork calculations.

inputs.label: Excavation Depth
inputs.businessContext: Depth of excavation. Critical for volume and stability analysis.

inputs.label: Slope Angle
inputs.businessContext: Angle of excavation sides for safety and volume adjustment. OSHA standards recommend max 34° for Type A soil.

inputs.label: Soil Type
inputs.businessContext: Soil classification per OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart P. Affects slope requirements and volume.

inputs.label: Swell Factor
inputs.businessContext: Percentage increase in volume after excavation due to soil loosening. Typical values: 20-30% for common earth.

inputs.label: Compaction Factor
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of original density achieved after compaction. ASTM D698 standard.

thresholds.depth > 5: Deep excavation warning: additional shoring may be required.
thresholds.slopeAngle > 45: Steep slope warning: risk of collapse.
thresholds.swellFactor > 30: High swell factor: consider material handling costs.

formulas.slopeAdjustedVolume: bankVolume + (0.5 * depth * depth * (length + width) * tan(slopeAngle * PI / 180))

suggestedActions: if slopeAngle > 45 then 'Flatten slope or use benching'
if swellFactor > 30 then 'Review material handling plan'
